observation: K508 root cause diagnosis: the K485A Comet Bridge content.js attached a keydown listener to a specific DOM element found by CSS selector. On Perplexity (React SPA), two failure modes: (1) selector no longer matches the active input element after Perplexity DOM changes; (2) the re-fired synthetic KeyboardEvent with _cometBridgeRefired=true is not processed by React's synthetic event system. DevTools confirmed: 19 requests to perplexity.ai, ZERO to localhost:7712/enrich — the intercept path was completely dead.

Critical MV3 distinction: ISOLATED world content scripts (default) have access to chrome.* APIs but cannot override window.fetch as seen by page code. MAIN world content scripts (world: 'MAIN') share the page's JavaScript context so window.fetch override works — but they CANNOT access chrome.runtime, chrome.storage, or any chrome.* APIs. This distinction is load-bearing for any extension that needs to both intercept page network calls AND call extension APIs.

The canonical pattern for MAIN world ↔ extension communication in MV3: injected.js (MAIN) sends window.postMessage({__namespace: 'request', requestId, payload}) to window. content.js (ISOLATED) listens via window.addEventListener('message', ...), calls chrome.runtime.sendMessage, gets result, and posts {__namespace: 'response', requestId, result} back. injected.js resolves a Promise keyed by requestId. Both scripts declared in manifest with run_at: document_start.

Async fetch interception requires a request-ID map to handle concurrent in-flight requests. Pattern: _nextId counter + _pending Map<requestId, {resolve, timer}>. Each intercepted fetch generates a unique ID, stores a resolve/reject + timeout handle in _pending, posts the ID with the message, and resolves when the response comes back with the matching ID. clearTimeout(pending.timer) on response. Essential when multiple queries can be submitted in rapid succession.

Robust fetch body parsing for content-modification intercept: handle (a) init.body as string, Blob, ArrayBuffer, or null; (b) input as Request object (requires clone().text()); (c) JSON.parse failure → pass through unmodified. Never await the body of the original Request without cloning first (reading a Request body is destructive). For reconstruction, rebuild from url string rather than mutating the Request object.

K508 cannot confirm the exact Perplexity API endpoint at implementation time — requires Phase A browser test (DevTools Console) to identify the real URL path and request body field names. injected.js ships with DEBUG=true and a broad INTERCEPT_PATH pattern (/api|rest|ask|query|perplexity/i) plus comprehensive body-key logging. Founder runs Phase A, identifies endpoint, then narrows INTERCEPT_PATH and sets DEBUG=false. Known candidate field names: query, messages[].content, prompt, search_query.

manifest.json pattern for MAIN world fetch interceptor: {matches: [...], js: ['injected.js'], run_at: 'document_start', world: 'MAIN'}. run_at: 'document_start' is required — injected.js must override window.fetch BEFORE the page's own scripts execute, otherwise the page's first fetch calls use the original fetch. The ISOLATED world bridge (content.js) also needs document_start to be ready for postMessage before any query is submitted.

Comet Bridge extension version bumped from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0 in manifest.json. Architecture change log: K485A (0.1.0) = DOM-replacement + keydown-intercept. K508 (0.2.0) = MAIN world fetch intercept via injected.js + ISOLATED world postMessage bridge via content.js. background.js unchanged except: save lastIntent to chrome.storage.local on successful enrichment (needed for popup display — popup.js already reads lastIntent but nothing was writing it).

LB Test Frame (lb-test-frame/extension/) now has two injection pathways: (1) Perplexity — MAIN world fetch interceptor (injected.js, new in K508) posts CB_LTF_ENRICH_REQUEST; content.js shows overlay, fetches enrichment from background, posts CB_LTF_ENRICH_RESPONSE with user's choice; injected.js modifies or passes through the request. (2) All other vendors (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot) — keep existing keydown-intercept + DOM-manipulation + submitBtn.click() path (those platforms are more DOM-stable).

For LB Test Frame's consent-based UX (overlay with 'Send original' / 'Send with LB context' buttons), the network-intercept approach is actually cleaner than the DOM approach: injected.js pauses the fetch Promise while content.js shows the overlay. User click resolves the Promise with useOriginal:true or useOriginal:false. No DOM manipulation of input fields needed, no submit button click needed. The overlay's ENRICH_TIMEOUT_MS (15s) matches the existing 15s auto-dismiss behavior.

MV3 service workers idle after 30 seconds and can take ~100ms to wake. content.js sends a PING_DAEMON message at script load time (document_start) to pre-warm the background service worker. This ensures the first enrichment request (which often comes within seconds of page load) does not stall during SW restart. Chrome.runtime.lastError is swallowed on the ping because the SW may not yet have its listener registered at the very first load.

lb-test-frame/electron/scripts/build-extension-zip.mjs INCLUDE_PATTERNS updated to include injected.js (K508 addition). archiver npm package installed to lb-test-frame/electron/. ZIP rebuilt at lb-test-frame/electron/dist/lb-test-frame-extension.zip (62 KB). lb-test-frame/extension/manifest.json version bumped to 1.1.0. This is the Chrome Web Store submission artifact; upload at https://chrome.google.com/webstore/devconsole/.

K508 was dispatched as a B124→B125 coherence test (HANDOFF_B124_TO_B125_K508_COHERENCE_TEST.md). B124 verbal scoping had 5 phases (A-E), fetch-interceptor pivot, Comet+Chrome+Edge test matrix, 4-6hr/$4 budget — all matched B125's independent draft. Key B125 addition: explicit MV3 technical precision (MAIN world + postMessage bridge, not 'service-worker interceptor') and the Phase A endpoint-identification step before implementation. Verdict: B124 was coherent at session end.
